name: lab
description: Homelab control plane — CLI, MCP server, and HTTP API for 22 services (Radarr, Sonarr, Plex, UniFi, Unraid, and more). Includes skills for onboarding new services and using the lab CLI.

skills.name: using-lab-cli
skills.description: Use when running lab CLI commands, operating any homelab service through the lab binary (Radarr, Sonarr, UniFi, Unraid, etc.), managing the lab MCP server, configuring credentials, scanning with lab extract, checking health with lab doctor, or scaffolding a new service.

skills.name: lab-service-onboarding
skills.description: Use when adding a new service integration to lab, finishing a partial one, migrating a dispatch stub to the full directory layout, or wiring any of the standard surfaces (lab-apis, dispatch, CLI, MCP, API, health, docs) into the repo's service contract.

skills.name: using-rmcp
skills.description: Use when building, modifying, or debugging MCP servers and clients in Rust using the rmcp crate — defining tools/resources/prompts, choosing transports, implementing ServerHandler or ClientHandler, sending progress notifications, or adding MCP tools.

skills.name: notebooklm
skills.description: Complete programmatic access to Google NotebookLM — create notebooks, add sources, generate all artifact types (podcast, video, quiz, flashcards, report, mind map), download results, and features not available in the web UI.

skills.name: gh-address-comments
skills.description: Use when addressing GitHub pull request review comments systematically with mandatory resolution tracking — fetches comments via gh CLI, creates task checklists, links commits to review threads, and blocks completion if unresolved threads remain.
